title: Legend of Pope Celestin and The Pistill of Susan (Horstmann selection)
summary: CME00087 is the checked-in copy of the University of Michigan encoding of two Middle English poem texts selected from Carl Horstmann's article 'Die Legenden von Celestin und Susanna,' Anglia 1 (1878). The encoding includes Legend of Pope Celestin on printed pages 67–85 and Susanna on pages 93–101, with Horstmann's textual notes, but omits his German Celestin introduction on pages 55–67, Susanna introduction on pages 85–93, and the separately printed metrical supplement on pages 390–391. Horstmann transcribed Celestin from the manuscript he called Laud L 70, now Bodleian Library MS. Laud Misc. 463, current ff. 118v–124va, and Susanna from the Vernon Manuscript, Bodleian Library MS. Eng. poet. a. 1, ff. 317ra–317vb. DIMEV records Celestin as a unique-witness poem and records five Susanna witnesses; the other four Susanna codices are collateral tradition, not sources of Horstmann's 1878 transcription. Complete public manuscript image sets are cached for Vernon and the collateral Morgan M.818 and Huntington HM 114 witnesses, with each work portion mapped inside the full published source. Laud Misc. 463 has only one unrelated public image, f. 115r; no complete public facsimile was found, so the whole physical codex and its target folios are specified separately in the Bodleian request route.

entities.label: General Corpus CME00087 XML artifact
entities.description: The exact XML file checked into the pinned CME submodule.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME electronic text CME00087
entities.description: A keyed electronic text of Horstmann's two poem transcriptions and their notes. The 2016 repository revision explicitly removed three prose fragments, leaving the poem-text selection rather than the complete article.

entities.label: Celestin and Susanna poem-text selection
entities.description: The edition-level selection formed by the two complete Middle English poem texts and retained textual notes, excluding Horstmann's two German introductions and later supplement.

entities.label: Legend of Pope Celestin
entities.description: An anonymous Middle English five-line-stanza saints' legend about a scholar who bargains with the devil, becomes pope, repents publicly, defeats the personified seven deadly sins, and is saved through divine and Marian mercy.

entities.label: The Pistill of Susan
entities.description: An anonymous alliterative Middle English stanzaic retelling of Susanna and the Elders. Five medieval witnesses are recorded; no direct descent among the surviving codices is asserted here.

entities.label: Carl Horstmann, Die Legenden von Celestin und Susanna (1878)
entities.description: The immediate source edition. Horstmann prints two separate poems from two separate Bodleian manuscripts, each after a substantial German linguistic and metrical introduction.

bibliographic.citation: Carl Horstmann, 'Die Legenden von Celestin und Susanna,' Anglia: Zeitschrift für englische Philologie 1 (1878): 55–101; separate supplement 'Ein Beitrag zu Celestin,' 390–391.
bibliographic.title: Die Legenden von Celestin und Susanna

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Anglia volume 1
entities.description: A complete 622-page scan of the carrier volume, including the entire article, all matter omitted from CME00087, and the later Celestin metrical supplement.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS. Laud Misc. 463
entities.description: The unique known witness of Legend of Pope Celestin, at current ff. 118v–124va. Horstmann cites its older designation Laud L 70 and the opening as fol. 118b.

notes: DIMEV associates the manuscript language with LALME/eLALME linguistic profile 7750, Worcestershire; this is witness-level scribal evidence, not proof of the poem's composition place.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian selected image for Laud Misc. 463
entities.description: A one-image Digital Bodleian object showing f. 115r. It does not overlap Celestin at ff. 118v–124va and is not a complete manuscript facsimile.
entities.reproduction_format: One selected color image derived from 35mm material

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS. Eng. poet. a. 1 (Vernon Manuscript)
entities.description: Horstmann's direct Susanna source at ff. 317ra–317vb. The late-fourteenth-century manuscript is physically incomplete, but its current complete published digital object exposes every cataloged surface in a 710-canvas sequence.

physical_description.extent: Current catalog: viii + 342 + i leaves, incomplete; originally viii + 413. Susanna occupies f. 317r–v.
physical_description.format: Large-format manuscript; Susanna is in three columns with stanzaic bracketing and red and blue decoration.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian complete facsimile of the Vernon Manuscript
entities.description: The complete 710-canvas published color object, including upper and lower covers, flyleaves, all surviving leaves, and a final spine/lower-cover canvas.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 / 710 color JPEG access derivatives

entities.label: British Library Add MS 22283 (Simeon Manuscript)
entities.description: A collateral Susanna witness at ff. 125vb–126rc, not a source of Horstmann's Vernon transcription.

entities.label: British Library Cotton MS Caligula A II
entities.description: A collateral, acephalous Susanna witness at current ff. 3r–5r. DIMEV states that stanzas 1–8 are absent because leaves are missing from the beginning of this manuscript unit; a nineteenth-century transcript is BL Egerton MS 2257, ff. 133r–136v.

date_statements.label: target manuscript unit
date_statements.value: second half of the fifteenth century

physical_description.extent: The current composite binding has 210 folios in two historical units; Susanna occupies ff. 3r–5r in the first unit.

entities.label: Morgan Library & Museum MS M.818
entities.description: A collateral complete Susanna witness at ff. 1r–5r in a 54-leaf paper miscellany.

physical_description.format: One column, 31–38 lines; Anglicana script; old vellum binding lined with canvas
notes: The item page gives England, Lincolnshire, while its prose note says northern England, possibly Yorkshire; both repository descriptions are retained without forcing a single composition claim.

entities.label: Morgan MS M.818 complete published image inventory
entities.description: All 111 public low-resolution images: front cover, inside front cover, every leaf recto and verso, and inside back cover. The provider does not expose the spine or exterior back cover.

entities.label: Huntington Library MS HM 114
entities.description: A collateral Susanna witness at ff. 184v–190v in a 325-leaf Middle English miscellany.

physical_description.support: Paper with parchment outer and center bifolia
physical_description.extent: 325 leaves plus four parchment flyleaves; Susanna occupies ff. 184v–190v.

entities.label: Huntington complete digital facsimile of MS HM 114
entities.description: The complete 679-canvas provider sequence, including current and former covers, spine, flyleaves, removed binding material, all manuscript leaves, and the Susanna target.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 / 679 color JPEG access derivatives

entities.label: Russell A. Peck, The Pistil of Swete Susan (1991)
entities.description: A later public METS scholarly edition and introduction useful for comparison. It is not upstream of CME00087 or Horstmann's 1878 Vernon transcription.

bibliographic.citation: Russell A. Peck, ed., 'The Pistil of Swete Susan,' in Heroic Women from the Old Testament in Middle English Verse (Kalamazoo: Medieval Institute Publications, 1991), 73–108.
bibliographic.title: The Pistil of Swete Susan
bibliographic.container_title: Heroic Women from the Old Testament in Middle English Verse

rights.component: Underlying medieval Middle English literary content
rights.basis: The two works and their manuscript witnesses are medieval and centuries outside copyright term; this does not grant rights in modern photographs, encodings, or editions.

rights.component: U-M electronic text and public item representation
rights.basis: The U-M CME collection applies the No Copyright–United States statement to its public-domain collection materials.
The article was first published in 1878, before 1930.

notes: Obtain clarification before redistributing the complete image set.

rights.component: 1991 edition as distributed in the current METS digital edition and PDF
rights.basis: METS states that current digital editions are for individual use only and asks users to contact the Executive Director to reprint digital text; its separate CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 statement applies to XML.

editorial_practices.description: The encoding retains the two poem texts and textual notes but removes Horstmann's two substantial German prose introductions and does not include the later pp. 390–391 metrical supplement.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Hooks on d, t, g, and k; strokes over h; ampersand; Jhu
editorial_practices.representation: Italic e for hooks and strokes, italic and for ampersand, and Jesu for Jhu
editorial_practices.description: Horstmann's opening Celestin note explicitly states these transformations. The XML retains the edition's italic HI markup; those italics therefore mark editorial expansion, not manuscript emphasis.
editorial_practices.scope: Legend of Pope Celestin, printed pp. 67–85

editorial_practices.representation: Horstmann comments on manuscript colons, sometimes moves or interprets them, and sets the long fifth Celestin line with a central colon; the source manuscript's exact signs cannot be reconstructed from normalized XML alone.
editorial_practices.description: The edition preserves useful observations about the Celestin stanza's graphic arrangement while making editorial typographic decisions.

editorial_practices.representation: Celestin has 735 L in 146 LG because LG 44 joins two five-line stanzas; Susanna has 365 L in 29 LG because the short line 'So ffre' is merged into the preceding L and the final prayer couplet has its own LG.
editorial_practices.description: XML LG and L counts are encoding metrics rather than normalized stanza or line counts. DIMEV reports 147 five-line Celestin stanzas and twenty-eight thirteen-line Susanna stanzas; Horstmann's Susanna print also has a final two-line prayer.

editorial_practices.representation: Editorial note stating that one or two stanzas of Envy's speech are missing
editorial_practices.description: The note supports a textual lacuna but does not identify a missing leaf in the present Laud Misc. 463 codex.

evidence.citation: CME00087 XML in the pinned CME submodule, structurally inspected 2026-07-12.
evidence.locator: BODY/DIV1[1–2], HEAD, LG, L, NOTE, PB, MILESTONE, TEMPHEAD/REVDESCR
evidence.summary: Confirms the two-work boundary, titles, incipits and explicits, 2016 removal of three prose fragments, exact XML metrics, printed-page references, manuscript milestones, retained notes, and italic expansion markup.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English item CME00087 and collection access statement.
evidence.summary: Identifies the public electronic text and the collection's No Copyright–United States access statement.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive, complete PDF and metadata for Anglia volume 1 (1878), item angliazeitschrif01tbuoft.
evidence.locator: 622 PDF pages; title page; Horstmann article PDF pp. 67–113; encoded poem runs PDF pp. 79–97 and 105–113; supplement at printed pp. 390–391
evidence.summary: Confirms complete carrier-volume coverage, title-page imprint, article and poem boundaries, the excluded introductions and supplement, and the provider's No Copyright–United States metadata label.

evidence.citation: Direct textual and visual audit of Carl Horstmann, 'Die Legenden von Celestin und Susanna,' Anglia 1 (1878): 55–101, and 'Ein Beitrag zu Celestin,' 390–391.
evidence.summary: Confirms Horstmann's source statements, poem ranges, plot and formal descriptions, historical dialect and authorship conjectures, expansion practices, the Celestin lacuna note, and the fact that CME00087 omits both introductions and the later separate Song to Our Lady supplement.

evidence.citation: Digital Index of Middle English Verse, version 1.0, records 352 and 5607 and linked manuscript/bibliography records.
evidence.locator: data/Records.xml record-352 and record-5607; data/Manuscripts.xml BodLauMis463 and BodEngPoeta1; data/Bibliography.xml Horstmann1878b
evidence.summary: Identifies Celestin as DIMEV 352/IMEV 195 with one witness at ff. 118v–124va and 147 five-line aaabb stanzas; identifies Susanna as DIMEV 5607/IMEV 3553 with twenty-eight thirteen-line ababababcdddc alliterative stanzas and five witnesses; crosswalks Laud L 70 to Laud Misc. 463 and cites Horstmann's exact pages.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Digital Bodleian selected-image object and exact catalog link for MS. Laud Misc. 463.
evidence.summary: Supplies the current shelfmark, late-fourteenth-century date, parchment support, exact catalog route, and CC BY-NC 4.0 attribution; proves that the public image object is partial and has zero Celestin overlap.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries catalog and Digital Bodleian IIIF manifest for MS. Eng. poet. a. 1, the Vernon Manuscript.
evidence.summary: Provides the current shelfmark, 1381–1400 date and c. 1390 note, tentative West Midlands origin, physical extent and incompleteness, material description, 710-canvas object, and CC BY-NC 4.0 attribution.

evidence.citation: Inventory and representative-image audit of the complete Vernon Manuscript IIIF bundle.
evidence.summary: Confirms complete provider-object coverage, exact target start/end canvases, color 1600-pixel derivatives, the three-column decorated stanza layout, and the boundary shared with the following Long Charter on f. 317v.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries Imaging Services, Order images and Copyright and reproducing images.
evidence.summary: Provides the mediated image-order route and distinguishes noncommercial or academic use from publication and other permission-controlled uses.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Add MS 22283 (Simeon Manuscript).
evidence.summary: Identifies the late-fourteenth-century parchment manuscript and its complete, rubricated Pistill of Susan locus; it is a collateral witness, not Horstmann's Vernon source.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Cotton MS Caligula A II and component ff. 3–139.
evidence.locator: Susanna ff. 3r–5r; parent physical object 210 folios
evidence.summary: Identifies the second-half-fifteenth-century first manuscript unit, the imperfect Susanna locus, current 210-folio composite binding, paper support, and physical request route.

evidence.citation: British Library Digitisation Services, imaging terms, online request form, and current rate card.
evidence.summary: Supports quote-dependent complete-object photography requests and the separation of delivery from publication permission.

evidence.citation: The Morgan Library & Museum, Miscellany (MS M.818) manuscript record and complete image sequence.
evidence.locator: 54 leaves; Epistle of Susannah ff. 1r–5r; 111 numbered images
evidence.summary: Supplies the shelfmark, 1425–1450 date, place statements, contents, material and binding description, exact target locus, and complete published image sequence from front cover through inside back cover.

evidence.citation: Inventory, ZIP-integrity, and target-image audit of the Morgan MS M.818 complete published image sequence.
evidence.summary: Confirms every provider-published image was retained, the exact first and last target pages, color low-resolution downloads, complete leaf coverage, and the absence of provider images for the spine and exterior lower cover.

evidence.citation: The Morgan Library & Museum, Terms and Conditions and Imaging & Rights.
evidence.summary: Allows website-image downloads for personal, educational, or noncommercial use with specified attribution and unchanged images; directs publication and commercial uses to Imaging and Rights and bars unauthorized dissemination.

evidence.citation: Huntington Library catalog and digital object for mssHM 114.
evidence.summary: Resolves former Phillipps/Cheltenham 8252 to current HM 114, dates the manuscript to the mid-fifteenth century, gives the complete physical description and target locus, and links the entire manuscript digital object.

evidence.citation: Inventory and representative-image audit of the complete Huntington HM 114 IIIF bundle.
evidence.summary: Confirms all canvases, target boundaries, adjacent works, binding surfaces, and color 1600-pixel derivatives; the first and last target canvases were visually inspected.

evidence.citation: The Huntington, Library Rights and Permissions.
evidence.summary: Leaves copyright analysis to the researcher and supplies no object-specific open license for HM 114 photography; the exact IIIF manifest has license:null.

evidence.citation: Russell A. Peck, ed., Heroic Women from the Old Testament in Middle English Verse (1991), current METS digital edition and complete downloadable PDF.
evidence.locator: Introduction printed pp. 73–81 / PDF pp. 74–82; poem printed pp. 82–93 / PDF pp. 83–94; notes printed pp. 94–108 / PDF pp. 95–109
evidence.summary: Provides a later public comparison edition, anonymous-author cataloging, biblical and alliterative classification, discussion of the five-witness tradition, and a readable text not ancestral to CME00087.

evidence.citation: Middle English Texts Series, Copyright & Citation.
evidence.summary: States that current digital editions are for individual use only and require contact for reprinting; separately licenses METS XML as CC BY-NC-SA 4.0.

open_questions.question: Can a complete color reproduction of Laud Misc. 463 be obtained and licensed for the project's private research and possible public-reference uses?
next_steps: Request the complete current physical codex with every surface, not only Celestin.
Name current ff. 118v–124va as the target mapping inside the full delivery.
Obtain written terms for local caching, Git-repository publication, noncommercial display, and commercial print-on-demand research context before redistributing any supplied image.

open_questions.question: Can complete color reproductions of Add MS 22283 and Cotton MS Caligula A II be obtained and licensed?
next_steps: Request each complete current physical object through BL Digitisation Services.
Map Susanna at Add MS 22283 ff. 125vb–126rc and Cotton Caligula A II ff. 3r–5r separately.
Confirm delivery, publication, repository-display, and commercial-use terms in writing.

open_questions.question: Does codicological inspection of Laud Misc. 463 explain Horstmann's reported loss of one or two Celestin stanzas after line 470?
next_steps: Inspect the complete codex around the line-470 locus after a full facsimile is obtained.
Distinguish missing text in an exemplar from physical loss or scribal omission in the current codex.

open_questions.question: Can Morgan provide images of the M.818 spine and exterior lower cover omitted from the otherwise complete public image sequence?
next_steps: Ask Imaging and Rights whether those exterior views already exist or may be commissioned.
Retain the present ZIP as complete only relative to the 111-image published inventory.
